hypothesis: If a 426M-parameter hybrid Transformer–Mamba encoder–decoder is pretrained jointly on seven molecular textual representations using masked-language modeling for alignment and decoder reconstruction, then it will learn a unified latent space that aligns modalities, improves property prediction across small molecules, polymers, and electrolytes, and enables high-fidelity translation among representations because multimodal supervision and the hybrid architecture capture complementary structural information shared across notations.
